summary: Stunning soundboard capture of a high‑energy late‑1977 show, highlighted by fiery Texas‑themed set and powerful drum jams.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard transfer with crystal‑clear stereo separation that lets every instrument shine. The performance bursts with classic late‑’77 energy – a Texas‑centric first set, soaring Jerry solos, tight Bob/Garcia interplay and a massive drum showdown that fuels the second half. Aside from an early “Good Lovin'” skip (now patched), the show is consistently vibrant.

ai_rating.rationale: Both the musical performance and the soundboard transfer are praised by multiple reviewers; only a minor technical glitch is noted, leading to an overall rating in the exceptional range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great stereo image and instrument clarity; one brief skip on Good Lovin' that has been repaired by the community.

standout_songs: Brokedown Palace > Playing in the Band (reprise)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set leans on a fun Texas‑town theme with high energy; second set expands into extended jams, highlighted by an explosive pre‑drums section and a three‑minute drum feature that feeds into Samson & The Wheel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for “blowing brain cells” with inventive phrasing and strong interaction with Bob in the pre‑drums section.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for tight interplay with Jerry during the pre‑drums jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as a massive, three‑minute “big boy” drum segment that powers the second set.
